amendments.body: Fix the `BeadStarted` journal dedupe collision introduced by allowing distinct named runs to share a `started_at`. `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:469-482` now lets `run-3` start even when `run-2` already finalized at the same timestamp, but `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:448-452` still records the start event as only `project={project_id}`. Because `src/adapters/fs.rs:2452-2457` treats `(timestamp, event_type, bead_id, details)` as the dedupe key, two same-project starts at the same timestamp collapse into one journal row and the later retry disappears from the milestone audit trail. Include `run_id`/`plan_hash` in `BeadStarted` details or otherwise widen the dedupe key for start events.
amendments.rationale: The task’s goal is to audit every Ralph attempt for a bead. The lineage file keeps both runs, but the journal silently loses one of the now-supported same-timestamp retries.

payload.summary: Reviewed the committed milestone-lineage changes in `model.rs`, `service.rs`, and `fs.rs` and ran targeted `cargo test` filters for `started_at`, `completion_journal_dedupe`, `find_runs_for_bead`, and `update_task_run`. I found one correctness gap: `BeadStarted` journal dedupe still collapses distinct same-timestamp retries from the same project because the start-event details do not carry run-level identity.
